description_long: This object is a rugged, military-style handheld radio transceiver, featuring a robust olive-green casing with a rectangular profile. The front face is equipped with a blue digital display screen, a numeric keypad, and several function buttons, all arranged in a grid below the screen. At the top, there are two prominent antennae—one black and cylindrical, the other gold and metallic—alongside a rotary knob for tuning or power. The casing appears to be made of durable plastic or composite material, designed to withstand harsh environments. The back is plain with a warning label, and the sides are slim with minimal protrusions. This device is typically used for secure communication in urban or field operations.
description: A rugged olive-green handheld radio transceiver with a digital display, keypad, and dual antennas, designed for secure communication.
description_view_0: Rear view showing a plain olive-green back with a warning label and the tops of the antennas visible.
description_view_1: Side profile revealing the slim, rectangular shape and the protruding black antenna at the top.
description_view_2: Front view displaying the blue digital screen, keypad, function buttons, speaker grill, and both antennas.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile, again showing the slim form and the black antenna at the top.
